# Revenue ledger

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> `test/stories/revenue_ledger_story_test.exs`. Every scenario below is an ExUnit test driving the
> real Codemojex game, so the catalogue cannot drift from the code.

**14 scenarios.**

## Scenario: the seed is booked as a negative house debit, wrapped atomically with the games-row write

- **Given** a Golden Room with virtual_deposit 1000 💎 (the seed = 100 keys)
- **When** the golden game forms (the first join seeds the pool)
- **Then** the house holds a -100 keys deposit_seed row and the pool holds 1000 💎
- **And** the house balance for the game is negative at this instant — the ledger admits it (no non-negative CHECK, D-1)

## Scenario: deposit-recovery-band buy-ins each credit the house the full fee with no pool move

- **Given** a Golden Room (entry_fee 8, start_threshold 2)
- **When** members 1..2 buy in (the deposit-recovery band)
- **Then** the house gained +8 keys per member (deposit_recovery), the pool is unchanged, each player's keys fell by 8
- **And** the house NET after the 2 recoveries equals Σ recovery − seed (16 − 100 = −84, the zero-loss made explicit)

## Scenario: a first-mover credits the house fee − pool_keys and the pool the same pool_keys ×10, summing to the fee

- **Given** a Golden Room past its gather threshold (2 recovery members in)
- **When** the first-mover (member 3) buys in
- **Then** the house gained +(8 − 4) = 4 keys (revenue) and the pool rose by 4×10 = 40 💎 — the SAME pool_keys both sides
- **And** the house credit + the pool portion sum, IN KEYS, to the 8-key fee exactly (4 + 4 == 8, zero dust)

## Scenario: a buy-in beyond the first-mover band credits the house the full fee with no pool move

- **Given** a Golden Room with the recovery band + the single first-mover filled (3 members in)
- **When** member 4 (ordinal > start_threshold + first_movers) buys in
- **Then** the house gained a +8 keys revenue row (member 4's full fee) and the pool is unchanged

## Scenario: for any sequence of buy-ins the three-term keys identity holds over three observable columns

- **Given** a Golden Room and four members spanning all three bands + the seed
- **When** all four buy in (members 1..4: recovery, recovery, first-mover, full-revenue)
- **Then** Σ(player key debits) == Σ(house key credits, excl. the deposit legs) + Σ(pool key portions) — exactly
- **And** the identity is proven by conservation over the three columns, explicitly NOT by Σ all-ledger-rows = 0 (D-3)

## Scenario: a voided Golden Room books exactly one +seed reclaim, idempotent under the close lock, no refund

- **Given** a never-fills Golden Room (deadline already past) that took 1 recovery buy-in
- **When** close_void fires (and re-fires on a second tick)
- **Then** the house holds exactly one +100 deposit_reclaim, the net is Σ kept fees, no player is refunded, the second tick books ZERO further rows

## Scenario: the reconciliation read's house figure equals the conservation figure cm.5 leaves implicit

- **Given** a Golden Room with four members across the bands
- **When** they all buy in and finance reads revenue_breakdown(game)
- **Then** the per-game revenue (recovery + revenue rows) equals Σ fee_i − Σ pool_💎_i/10 — the cm.5-only conservation figure
- **And** house_balance groups by currency — keys now, a cm.7 stars row would sum into its own bucket with no read change

## Scenario: a buy-in's player debit, pool increment, and house credit are all present together

- **Given** a Golden Room past gather so a first-mover moves all three columns at once
- **When** the first-mover buys in (one Repo.transaction under the games-row lock)
- **Then** the player keys debit AND the prize_pool increment AND the revenue_ledger credit are all present

## Scenario: the ledger balances by conservation over three columns, NOT by a zero row-sum, with the deferred rung named

- **Given** a Golden Room with the seed + buy-ins (the player debit is a bare keys column; the pool is a games column)
- **When** the revenue_ledger rows for the game are summed naively
- **Then** the naive single-table row-sum is NOT zero — it is the house balance, not the system total (the honesty statement, D-3)

## Scenario: cm.6 writes only the new revenue_ledger table — the cm.5 player/pool figures are untouched

- **Given** a Golden Room and two recovery buy-ins (the cm.5 path)
- **When** the members buy in under cm.6
- **Then** the cm.5 figures are exactly as cm.5 produced them — the pool at the seed, the players debited 8 (the house credit is disjoint)

## Scenario: the revenue_ledger holds a source + currency dimension with a signed delta and no non-negative CHECK

- **Given** the revenue_ledger founded this rung, with one golden game's rows
- **When** its shape is inspected (structure, not new behaviour)
- **Then** it carries account=platform + currency=keys + a signed delta (a negative seed row exists — no non-negative CHECK, D-1)

## Scenario: PROPERTY: the keys-exact partition house_cut + pool_keys == fee holds across the fee×rev% grid (incl. div(pool,9)≠div(pool,10))

- **Given** the partition grid: entry_fee_keys × revenue_pct × ordinals spanning all three bands
- **When** each grid cell computes the house cut the production buy-in computes and the oracle pool keys
- **Then** in-band house_cut + pool_keys == fee exactly; out-of-band house_cut == fee; the pool is the oracle ×10
- **And** the grid actually exercised a divergence cell where div(pool, 9) ≠ div(pool, 10) (else the property is blind to M5)

## Scenario: PROPERTY: the three-term conservation Σ identity holds over real buy-ins across configs

- **Given** a grid of golden-room configs spanning the bands, incl. a first-mover pool of 90 (div9≠div10)
- **When** each config runs member_count real buy-ins into a fresh golden room
- **Then** for every config Σ(player debits) == Σ(house recovery+revenue credits) + Σ(pool key portions)

## Scenario: PROPERTY: the reconciliation figure equals the cm.5-only conservation figure across configs

- **Given** the same config grid (each closes to a per-game revenue_breakdown read)
- **When** each config runs its buy-ins and finance reads revenue_breakdown(game)
- **Then** for every config the explicit per-game revenue == Σ fee_i − Σ (pool 💎 from buy-ins / 10)
